pregame_model_read.pre_bet_thesis: Strider 7+ at +152 was treated as a controlled redemption spot with plus-money median value, but carried an explicit prior burn penalty tied to CSW collapse and fastball/slider conversion instability.

what_worked: The model correctly marked Strider as volatile and avoided an 8+ ladder.
Slider whiff quality was usable at 41% whiffs and 36% CSW.
First-pitch strike rate was strong at 71%, so the issue was not simply falling behind.
what_failed: Fastball velocity was down 1.6 mph versus season average and generated only 2 whiffs on 14 swings.
Strider allowed 3 home runs and 7 earned runs, cutting off inning runway at 3.0 IP.
Overall CSW reached only 28%, below the 31%+ green zone for a 7+ strikeout path.
The same burn family reappeared: fastball whiff/finish instability and damage risk prevented threshold pursuit.
risk_notes: This was both a quality and runway failure. Strider had only 68 pitches and 17 batters faced because run prevention collapsed.
The slider was fine, but the fastball no longer supported the historical K ceiling in this start.
Until fastball velo/whiff returns, Strider should no longer be treated as a plus-money median auto-play.
model_evaluation.patch_recommendation: Promote STRIDER_BURN_RETEST to STRIDER_FASTBALL_DECAY_LOCKOUT: require pregame or live confirmation that four-seam velocity is near season baseline and early fastball whiff rate is active before taking 7+ markets. If fastball velo is -1.0 mph or worse and CSW <31% through two innings, no add-ons and consider future downgrade.
